narrative: Dear [Student's Name], I know this rejection letter stings—pouring your heart into law school dreams only to have the door close like this feels deeply unfair, and I'm truly sorry you're facing it. Our prediction model looked at your profile and saw a 48% chance of struggling to pass the bar exam down the line, which tipped the scales against admission. What stood out most was your Year 3 decile of 1.0, the lowest rung in your school, signaling to the model that your performance dipped significantly later on, pulling hardest toward this tough outlook. Right behind that was your LSAT score of 31, which falls on the lower side and reinforced concerns about handling the intense demands ahead. Your Year 1 decile of 1.0 added to that early picture of academic challenges, making it three strikes from your school rankings alone. Coming from a lower-middle family income bracket, the model worried you might lack some of the financial cushions others have for extra tutoring or resources during bar prep. Your undergraduate GPA of 3.2, while solid, didn't quite offset those patterns, nudging the prediction further toward risk. Even factors like being a white male in a full-time program played small roles in painting this overall cautious image, as the model weighs how similar profiles have fared historically. It's not that you're incapable—far from it—but the combination of these elements created a story of potential hurdles that the school couldn't ignore. This isn't the end of your path; many with similar starts have pivoted, strengthened weak spots like test prep or later grades, and succeeded elsewhere. You've got resilience in you—use this as fuel to rewrite your story. Keep pushing forward.
